Package space.sunqian.fs.base.number

Interface NumFormatter

public interface NumFormatter
Formatter for number. The implementation of this interface is immutable and thread-safe.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static @Nonnull NumFormatter
    common()
    Returns a common NumFormatter which uses Object.toString() to format the number and NumKit.toNumber(CharSequence, Class) to parse the string.
    @Nonnull String
    format(@Nonnull Number num)
    Formats the given number.
    default @Nullable String
    formatSafe(@Nullable Number num)
    Formats the given number.
    static @Nonnull NumFormatter
    ofPattern(@Nonnull String pattern)
    Returns a new NumFormatter with the given number format pattern.
    static @Nonnull NumFormatter
    ofSupplier(@Nonnull Supplier<? extends @Nonnull NumberFormat> supplier)
    Returns a new NumFormatter with the given number format supplier.
    <T> T
    parse(@Nonnull CharSequence numStr, @Nonnull Class<T> numType)
    Parses the given number string to an instance of the specified number type.
    default <T> T
    parseSafe(@Nullable CharSequence numStr, @Nonnull Class<T> numType)
    Parses the given number string to an instance of the specified number type.

  • Method Details

    • common

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull NumFormatter common()
      Returns a common NumFormatter which uses Object.toString() to format the number and NumKit.toNumber(CharSequence, Class) to parse the string. Note the returned instance is singleton.
      Returns:
      a common NumFormatter which uses Object.toString() to format the number and NumKit.toNumber(CharSequence, Class) to parse the string

    • ofSupplier

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull NumFormatter ofSupplier(@Nonnull @Nonnull Supplier<? extends @Nonnull NumberFormat> supplier)
      Returns a new NumFormatter with the given number format supplier.
      Parameters:
      supplier - the number format supplier
      Returns:
      a new NumFormatter with the given number format supplier

    • ofPattern

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull NumFormatter ofPattern(@Nonnull @Nonnull String pattern)
      Returns a new NumFormatter with the given number format pattern.

      By default, this method uses DecimalFormat and ThreadLocal to support multi-threading.

      Parameters:
      pattern - the number format pattern
      Returns:
      a new NumFormatter with the given number format pattern

    • format

      @Nonnull @Nonnull String format(@Nonnull @Nonnull Number num) throws NumException
      Formats the given number.
      Parameters:
      num - the given number to format
      Returns:
      the formatted string
      Throws:
      NumException - if any error occurs

    • formatSafe

      @Nullable default @Nullable String formatSafe(@Nullable @Nullable Number num) throws NumException
      Formats the given number. If the given number is null, or an exception thrown during formating, returns null.
      Parameters:
      num - the given number to format, can be null
      Returns:
      the formatted string, or null if the given number is null or an exception thrown
      Throws:
      NumException - if any error occurs

    • parse

      @Nonnull <T> T parse(@Nonnull @Nonnull CharSequence numStr, @Nonnull @Nonnull Class<T> numType) throws NumException
      Parses the given number string to an instance of the specified number type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the number type
      Parameters:
      numStr - the given number string to parse
      numType - the specified number type
      Returns:
      the parsed number instance
      Throws:
      NumException - if any error occurs

    • parseSafe

      @Nullable default <T> T parseSafe(@Nullable @Nullable CharSequence numStr, @Nonnull @Nonnull Class<T> numType) throws NumException
      Parses the given number string to an instance of the specified number type. If the given number string is null, or an exception thrown during parsing, returns null.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the number type
      Parameters:
      numStr - the given number string to parse, can be null
      numType - the specified number type
      Returns:
      the parsed number instance, or null if the given number string is null or an exception thrown
      Throws:
      NumException - if any error occurs
